item.title: Ba edge Navua 3-1 during penalties to win BiC Fiji FACT after 16-year wait
item.description: Ba edge Navua 3-1 during penalties to win BiC Fiji FACT after 16-year wait

item.title: Legge pleased as Drua Women secure home semi-final with big Reds win
item.description: Legge pleased as Drua Women secure home semi-final with big Reds win

item.title: Portugal held to a 0-0 draw by Colombia at FIFA World Cup
item.description: Portugal has been held to a 0-0 draw by Colombia in their final FIFA World Cup Group K match. Both teams failed to find the breakthrough after the match was locked at 0-0 at half-time.
item.content:encoded:
Portugal has been held to a 0-0 draw by Colombia in their final FIFA World Cup Group K match.

Both teams failed to find the breakthrough after the match was locked at 0-0 at half-time.

Meanwhile in other matches, DR Congo beat Uzbekistan 3-1, England defeated Panama 2-0, while Croatia edged Ghana 2-1 in their final FIFA World Cup group matches.

Today's FIFA World Cup action continues with Algeria taking on Austria and Jordan facing Argentina at 2pm.

item.title: England finish top After 2-0 win over Panama in FIFA World Cup clash
item.description: England has finished on top of Group L at the FIFA World Cup after beating Panama 2-0 in their final group match. The match was locked 0-0 at half-time before England came alive in the second half.
item.content:encoded:
England has finished on top of Group L at the FIFA World Cup after beating Panama 2-0 in their final group match.

The match was locked 0-0 at half-time before England came alive in the second half.

Jude Bellingham opened the scoring in the 62nd minute after which Harry Kane then doubled the lead five minutes later.

Croatia edged Ghana 2-1 to secure their place in the FIFA World Cup Round of 32

Meanwhile, today's FIFA World Cup action continues with Colombia taking on Portugal and DR Congo facing Uzbekistan at 11:30am, while Algeria meet Austria and Jordan play Argentina at 2pm.

item.title: Ba and Navua to face off in Fiji FACT final after 16 years
item.description: Ba and Navua will face each other in the BIC Fiji FACT final for the first time in 16 years when they meet at 3pm today at Subrail Park in Labasa. Navua will be looking to avenge their 2010 Fiji FACT final defeat to Ba, while the Men in Black will be hoping to lift the title for the first time since that victory after finishing runners-up in 2012 and 2013.
item.content:encoded:
Ba and Navua will face each other in the BIC Fiji FACT final for the first time in 16 years when they meet at 3pm today at Subrail Park in Labasa.

Navua will be looking to avenge their 2010 Fiji FACT final defeat to Ba, while the Men in Black will be hoping to lift the title for the first time since that victory after finishing runners-up in 2012 and 2013.

Ba booked their place in the final after edging defending champions Labasa 1-0 after extra time, while Navua came from behind to beat Lautoka 2-1 in their semi-final.

A large crowd is expected at Subrail Park as supporters from both districts turn out to cheer on their teams in what promises to be an exciting final.

item.title: Drua Women crush Reds 50-17 in dominant display
item.description: The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's team have secured a home semi-final after producing a dominant display to defeat the Reds 50-17, running in a series of impressive tries while showcasing their attacking flair and rock-solid defence. The Drua were in complete control during the opening half.
item.content:encoded:
The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's team have secured a home semi-final after producing a dominant display to defeat the Reds 50-17, running in a series of impressive tries while showcasing their attacking flair and rock-solid defence.

The Drua were in complete control during the opening half.

In the 28th minute, Drua crossed over for another try to give them a commanding 21-0 lead.

Qoro sliced through the Reds' defence before delivering a perfectly timed pass to Aqela Biutu, who finished the move to extend the advantage to 26-0.

The Reds finally found a breakthrough in the 38th minute with their first try of the match, reducing the deficit to 26-5.

However, the Drua responded immediately.

Applying relentless pressure, they forced another opening and crossed over in the 39th minute to head into halftime with a comfortable 33-5 lead.

The second half began with the Reds looking for a way back into the contest.

Eva Kapani, the Reds Prop, produced a strong line break shortly after the restart, but the Drua's defence held firm to deny the home side.

Both teams created attacking opportunities during the opening stages of the half, but handling errors and strong defensive efforts prevented either side from adding to the scoreboard.

The breakthrough eventually came in the 49th minute when Litiana Vueti scored another well-worked try, stretching the Drua's lead to 40-5.

The Drua continued to apply pressure in attack, although they lost possession after building momentum in the 60th minute.

Four minutes later, the Reds mounted several attacking phases deep inside the Drua's territory, Drua side's determined defence stood tall to keep them scoreless.

The Drua then launched one of the most impressive attacks of the match in the 67th minute.

After patiently building through 11 phases, they finally broke the Reds' defence to score another try and extend their lead to 47-5.

After more than 20 phases of relentless pressure in the 73rd minute, the Reds earned a penalty and eventually crossed for a try two minutes later to cut the score to 47-10.

They added another late try in the 79th minute to narrow the margin further to 47-17.

With the final play of the match, Qoro calmly slotted a penalty goal to bring up the half-century, sealing an emphatic 50-17 victory for the Drua Women.

The convincing win highlighted the Drua's clinical finishing, disciplined defence, and ability to dominate possession, as they outclassed the Reds from start to finish.

28 th minute McDonald's women scores another try to Robanakadavu to lead 21-0

29 minute Qoro splits the defense open, delivers a pass to Aqela Biutu to score another try to extend their lead 26-0.

38 th minute Reds score their first try in 38 th minute Drua lead 26-5.

39 th minute Drua scores another try to extend their lead after pressuring the Reds 33-5.

40th minute, Reds kicks off and Eva Kapani breaks the Reds line but Drua defense too strong for her.

46th minute both team attacking but not finishing as expected.

49th minute Litiana Vueti scores another try to extend their lead 40-5.

60th minute Drua attacking Reds line showing momentum and losses possesion.

64th minute reds launch multiple attack phases on Drua line and fail to score any points after another strong defense.

67th minute Drua causing havoc attacking the Reds line into the 11 phase and scores a try to extend their lead 47-5.

73th After 20 phases, the Reds attacked the Drua line they could not score but get another chance through a penalty.

item.title: Flying Fijians have four days to prepare for Wales Test
item.description: With a week to go till the Nations Championship, members of the FIJI Water Flying Fijians that were based in Fiji have already flo

item.title: Baulevu High School seal 1-0 win over Bhawani Dayal Arya
item.description: Baulevu High School has kicked off its Rewa Secondary Schools Football Association Under-17 Pool A campaign with a 1-0 win over Bhawani Dayal Arya College at Saraswati Manoca Ground. The lone goal of the match came in the 10th minute when Inia Robert T.R. found the back of the net to hand Baulevu all three points.
item.content:encoded:
Baulevu High School has kicked off its Rewa Secondary Schools Football Association Under-17 Pool A campaign with a 1-0 win over Bhawani Dayal Arya College at Saraswati Manoca Ground.

The lone goal of the match came in the 10th minute when Inia Robert T.R. found the back of the net to hand Baulevu all three points.

Despite Bhawani Dayal Arya College creating several opportunities, Baulevu's defence stood firm to secure the victory.

Meanwhile, Lomaivuna High School and Saraswati College played out a hard-fought 0-0 draw.

Source: Rewa Secondary School Football Association

item.title: Wales beat Barbarians 33-31 as North signs off in style

item.title: One-test All Black joins Welsh club
item.description: Another All Black is heading offshore.After seven seasons, Crusaders loose froward Cullen Grace will join Welsh club Scarlets afte
